title: [FIRRTL LowerTypes] Lower SubaccessOp
body:
As part of type lowering, FIRRTL `SubaccessOp` should be broken up into per-element reads fed into a mux tree. 

Roughly, this should look like the following output from the Scala FIRRTL Compiler:

Input FIRRTL IR:
```scala
circuit Foo:
  module Foo:
    input a: UInt<1>[4]
    input sel: UInt<2>
    output b: UInt<1>

    b <= a[sel]
```

Output Verilog (`firrtl -i Foo.fir && cat Foo.v`):
```verilog
module Foo(
  input        a_0,
  input        a_1,
  input        a_2,
  input        a_3,
  input  [1:0] sel,
  output       b
);
  wire  _GEN_1 = 2'h1 == sel ? a_1 : a_0;
  wire  _GEN_2 = 2'h2 == sel ? a_2 : _GEN_1;
  assign b = 2'h3 == sel ? a_3 : _GEN_2;
endmodule
```
